title: Unidentified Majoor species
content:
{{Top|fa|leg}} {{Otheruses|Unidentified species}} {{Conjecture|legends}} {{Species |image=[[File:Majoorans.png]] |name=Unidentified Majoor species |designation=[[Sentience/Legends|Sentient]][[Droids (1986) 3|''Droids'' (1986) 3]] |class= |subspecies= |races= |height=More than 1.67 [[Meter/Legends|meters]]Ambassador Zell, an adult member of the species, stood slightly taller than the protocol droid C-3PO, who was 1.67 meters tall according to the ''[[Star Wars Character Encyclopedia]]''. |length= |wingspan= |mass= |skincolor=[[Color/Legends|Light green]] |haircolor=Blue-black |feathers= |eyecolor=Black |distinctions=Three-fingered [[Limb/Legends|hands]], thick [[Mouth/Legends|lips]], round head |lifespan= |origin=[[Majoor]] |habitat= |diet= |language=[[Galactic Basic Standard/Legends|Basic]] }} {{Quote|Captain! I'm picking up a convoy of ships!''"''
''"''Aye! That'll be the peace convoy to Armath led by Ambassador Zell! Prepare to attack!|A crewman and the pirate Reddjak prepare to ambush Zell's convoy|}} A '''species''' of [[Sentience/Legends|sentient]], [[Color/Legends|green]]-skinned [[Humanoid/Legends|humanoids]] made up at least part of the population of the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Majoor]] in the [[Expansion Region/Legends|Expansion Region]]. Each member of the [[Species/Legends|species]] had two three-fingered [[Limb/Legends|hands]], a globular head, and thick [[Mouth/Legends|lips]]. The species had access to [[The galaxy/Legends|galactic]]-standard technology by the [[Time/Legends|time]] of the early [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]]. Their architecture featured dome- and saucer-shaped, white-colored structures, some held aloft by narrow towers. Their government supported a [[Fleet/Legends|fleet]] of [[Realspace/Legends|spacegoing]] [[Starship/Legends|vessels]], some of which were used to engage in [[Diplomat/Legends|diplomacy]] with neighboring peoples. To this end, the species also fielded [[Ambassador/Legends|ambassadors]]. During one such [[Peace mission to Armath|mission]], the Ambassador [[Zell]] led a [[Convoy/Legends|convoy]] to the planet [[Armath]] to discuss peace. However, the ships were attacked by the [[Pirate/Legends|pirate]] [[Captain/Legends|captain]] [[Reddjak]], who had been freed from [[Prisoner/Legends|incarceration]] on Majoor by Zell's own son, [[Llez]]. Llez had a child's romantic view of piracy, but the boy soon saw his mistake when he realized that Reddjak intended to attack his [[Parent/Legends|father's]] peace mission. With the help of his [[Droid/Legends|droid]] caretakers, [[C-3PO/Legends|C-3PO]] and [[R2-D2/Legends|R2-D2]], Llez saved his father's convoy and allowed Zell to lead a security team to [[Blood Brother|Reddjak's ship]] to overpower and apprehend the fugitive pirate. ==Biology and appearance== {{Quote|Listen, you little toadstool… I decide who's innocent or not on this ship! And you're either with me or against me!|Reddjak to Llez|}} [[File:Crying_Llez.png|thumb|left|upright|Tears formed in the eyes of members of the species when they were sad.]] At least some of the [[Sentience/Legends|sentient]] inhabitants of the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Majoor]] were members of a [[Species/Legends|species]] of [[Humanoid/Legends|humanoids]] with [[Color/Legends|light-green]] skin. Members of the species had four [[Limb/Legends|limbs]]: two legs, and two arms with three-fingered hands. While the [[Youngling/Legends|children]] were much shorter, the adults of the species could grow taller than 1.67 [[Meter/Legends|meters]]. Some individuals developed a noticeable paunch, but other members of the species were more trim. The species had at least one [[Gender/Legends|sex]], male. A defining characteristic of each of Majoor's green-skinned sentients was a round head almost perfectly spherical in shape. Only facial features broke up the head's smooth, curved surface. Two circular, black [[Eye/Legends|eyes]] peered from the center of the head from under black eyebrows; when opened wide, the eyes revealed white sclera as well. The eyes produced tears when a member of the species was saddened. Two thin nostrils slanted inward from a spot below the eyes; while some individuals had no appreciable [[Nose/Legends|nose]], others had a small protrusion in the vicinity of the nostrils. Thick lips framed a wide [[Mouth/Legends|mouth]], which housed a set of white, buck [[Tooth/Legends|teeth]]. A head of blue-black [[Hair/Legends|hair]] crowned the pate. ==Society and culture== {{Quote|Well, Scarlet Pirate… What would your family think of your joining old Reddjak?''"''
''"''There's only my dad… and he doesn't care much about me! He's only interested in running off on peace missions!|Reddjak and Llez|}} [[File:Majoor_bridge.png|thumb|right|Consular ships from Majoor held full crew complements.]] By circa [[15 BBY/Legends|15 BBY]],{{SW|url=news/the-droids-re-animated-part-1|series=The Droids Re-Animated|text=Part 1|nolive=1}} members of the species had access to technology analogous to that used by [[Realspace/Legends|spacefaring]] cultures in the rest of [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]]. For instance, children could learn to [[Pilot/Legends|pilot]] [[Speeder/Legends|speeders]], and adults fought with [[Blaster pistol/Legends|blaster pistols]]. The species had access to [[Starship/Legends|starship]] technology; [[Consular ship/Legends|one type]] of [[Consular ship/Legends|consular ship]] was wedge-shaped and colored gold; it featured a [[Bridge (starship)/Legends|bridge]] large enough for several crewmembers and the [[Captain/Legends|captain]], who sat in a round chair with controls in the armrests. Such ships were equipped for long-range communications. A small [[Shuttle/Legends|shuttle]] could fit aboard the consular ship; [[Shuttle/Legends|one type]] had gold-colored plating and triangular symbols on it. On Majoor, the species worked and lived in dome-shaped buildings that were white in color. Some of the structures supported a long, thin neck topped by a saucer-shaped tower. They kept [[Prisoner/Legends|prisoners]] in dome-shaped [[Prison/Legends|incarceration centers]], where they were watched over by [[droid guard]]s; such [[Droid/Legends|droids]] also ferried prisoners from one facility to another on [[droid guard ship]]s. Less-high-technology tools used by members of the species included toy [[Sword/Legends|swords]] for children, and valises and [[Backpack/Legends|backpacks]] to carry luggage. During the early [[Standard year/Legends|years]] of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]], some children were avid followers of a program called ''[[Space Pirates of the Galaxy]]''. At least some members of the species could speak [[Galactic Basic Standard/Legends|Basic]]. During that timeframe, the [[Clothing/Legends|garb]] of an [[Ambassador/Legends|ambassador]] from Majoor consisted of a purple [[Jumpsuit/Legends|jumpsuit]] with yellow trim, a light-blue sash, yellow epaulettes, a light-blue collar, medals pinned to the chest, and a light-blue [[Armor/Legends|helmet]]. The bridge crew of at least one consular vessel wore teal jumpsuits with helmets, sleeve trim, buttons, [[Belt/Legends|belts]], and [[Boot/Legends|boots]] of orange; they also sported yellow epaulettes and headsets. A child, in contrast, might have worn an orange jumpsuit with yellow buttons, a purple belt, and light-blue [[Glove/Legends|gloves]] and boots with a red-and-yellow-striped bandana tied around the head. Members of the species valued family, at least nominally, with [[Parent/Legends|parents]] taking care of their children. Indeed, single-parent households were not unheard of. At least some children had their own rooms, where they kept toys and decorated with posters. A [[Sleeper (furniture)|bunk bed]] was one possible [[Sleep/Legends|sleeping]] arrangement for children. Nevertheless, some occupations demanded a great deal of [[Time/Legends|time]] from adults; a [[Diplomat/Legends|diplomat]], for instance, was often expected to spend long periods away from home, sometimes leaving their families behind, and other times bringing them along. ==History== {{Quote|Firing all plasma torpedoes now, Captain!''"
"''Heh, heh…Just like shooting acid rats in a crater!|The two heads of Reddjak's Dyclops gunner during their attack on the peace convoy to Armath|}} [[File:Peace_mission_to_Almath.png|thumb|left|The peace mission to Armath comes under attack.]] The region of space containing Majoor was integrated into the wider [[Galaxy/Legends|galaxy]] by [[15,000 BBY/Legends|15,000 BBY]].''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' By the early years of the Galactic Empire, the inhabitants of Majoor had achieved spacefaring technology and made contact with other members of the [[galactic community]]. Their world fell within the [[Expansion Region/Legends|Expansion Region]] of the galaxy, and it had become a stop along the [[Hydian Way/Legends|Hydian Way]] [[Hyperspace route/Legends|hyperroute]] by [[3702 BBY]] During the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]], the planet fell within territory controlled by the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]]. By circa 15 BBY, Majoor's relations with [[Armath]], another Expansion Region planet, had soured. The governments of Majoor and Armath agreed to peace talks, and Majoor sent an Ambassador named [[Zell]], who had previously served as a [[Republic Space Ranger]], to lead the [[peace mission to Armath]]. Zell was quite wealthy, and he purchased two droids—the [[Protocol droid/Legends|protocol unit]] [[C-3PO/Legends|C-3PO]] and the [[R2-series astromech droid/Legends|astromech]] [[R2-D2/Legends|R2-D2]]—to care for and tutor his son, [[Llez]], whom he frequently left on Majoor while away on official business. During the droids' first [[Standard day/Legends|day]] on the job, the boy learned that the [[Pirate/Legends|pirate]] captain [[Reddjak]] had been arrested and was slated for incarceration in the [[Majoor penitentiary]]. With a child's storybook notion of pirates, born of a diet of [[Hologram/Legends|holo]] series such as ''Space Pirates of the Galaxy'', Llez stole a family [[Landspeeder/Legends|landspeeder]] to rescue Reddjak. At the prison, the boy [[Stun/Legends|stunned]] the [[Security droid/Legends|droid guards]] with a [[magno-neutralizer]] and freed the pirate. The fugitive and his rescuer escaped into [[Orbit/Legends|orbit]] on the prison ship and boarded [[Blood Brother|Reddjak's starship]]. C-3PO and R2-D2 came along as stowaways. In his zeal to win the approval of his idol, Llez revealed that he was the son of Ambassador Zell, and that his father was leading a peace [[Convoy/Legends|convoy]] to Almath. Aboard the pirate vessel, Reddjak made the youngling feel like a member of his [[Red Fury Brotherhood/Legends|crew]] of [[Alien/Legends|non-Humans]]. Only when the pirates intercepted the peace convoy to Armath did Llez realize their true intentions, but Reddjak ignored Llez's pleas to spare his father and ordered C-3PO and R2-D2—whom he mistook for members of his ship's droid complement—to take Llez below deck. As the convoy from Majoor found themselves helpless in the face of the pirates' [[Plasma torpedo/Legends|plasma torpedo]] attack, Llez, C-3PO, and R2-D2 overwhelmed the [[Dyclops]] [[Unidentified Dyclops pirate|pirate gunner]], and the [[Astromech droid/Legends|astromech]] detonated the [[Missile/Legends|missiles]] remotely before they could impact. Zell and [[Unidentified starship captain|one of his ships' captains]] led a security team to the pirate ship. There, Zell confronted Reddjak and defeated him, and the pirate was taken into custody. In the aftermath of the incident, Zell nearly sacked C-3PO and R2-D2 until Llez informed his father of how much the droids had aided him. The father also realized how he had neglected his son and vowed to take him along on diplomatic missions in the future. By [[0 ABY/Legends|0 ABY]], Majoor was part of the [[Majoor sector/Legends|Majoor sector]].{{SW|url=news/star-wars-the-essential-atlas-online-companion|text=''Star Wars: The Essential Atlas'' Online Companion|int=Star Wars: The Essential Atlas Online Companion}} Circa [[25 ABY/Legends|25 ABY]], Majoor fell within a region of the galaxy whose average population was between 500 million and 1 billion sentient inhabitants per [[Star system/Legends|star system]]. By [[137 ABY]], it was within territory controlled by the [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Sith Lord]] [[Darth Krayt]] as part of his [[Darth Krayt's Galactic Empire|Galactic Empire]]. ==Majoor inhabitants in the galaxy== {{Quote|Your father is away quite a bit, Sir?''"''
''"''When it comes to Dad, the words I know best are 'hello' and 'goodbye'!|C-3PO and Llez discuss Ambassador Zell's frequent trips offworld|}} By the rise of the Galactic Empire, the people of Majoor had full spacefaring capabilities, which allowed members of the green-skinned species who inhabited that world to travel throughout the galaxy. Even before the Empire's rise, at least one member of the species left Majoor to serve in the elite Republic Space Rangers. Around 15 BBY, the species' ambassadors were among those who regularly left their homeworld to engage with members of other societies. ==Behind the scenes== The unnamed species inhabiting Majoor features in the [[Droids (1986) 3|third issue]] of the [[Star Wars: Droids (1986)|''Star Wars: Droids'']] [[Star Wars comic books|comic book]], which was published in [[August]] [[1986]] by [[Marvel Comics]]' [[Star Comics]] imprint. The species and their planet were created by writer [[David Manak|Dave Manak]]. Their appearance was designed by the artists [[John Romita Sr.]] and [[George Roussos]], who respectively penciled and colored the issue. In an [[The Droids Re-Animated|article]] published via the [[Star Wars Blog|''Star Wars'' Blog]] in [[2013]], authors [[Abel G. Peña]] and [[Rich Handley]] summarize the events of ''Droids 3'', but they do not give the name of Llez and Zell's species. Peña later revealed that he and Handley simply had assumed that the name was obviously ''Majooran''.{{SW|url=news/viva-space-vegas-the-history-of-the-marvelous-wheel-part-2|series=Viva Space Vegas! The History of the Marvelous Wheel|text=Part 2|nolive=1}} ==Appearances== {{Mediacat|imagecat=Images of unidentified Majoor species}} *[[Droids (1986) 3|''Droids'' (1986) 3]] {{1st}} ==Sources== *{{SW|url=news/the-droids-re-animated-part-1|series=The Droids Re-Animated|text=Part 1|nolive=1}} ==Notes and references== {{Reflist}} [[Category:Expansion Region sentient species]] {{RelatedCategories |[[Category:Members of the unidentified Majoor species]] }}
